title.english: Kaitiakitanga of the Mangakōtukutuku Stream: Language of Guardianship
title.teReoMāori: Te Kaitiakitanga o Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku: Te Reo o te Tiaki

subject: Te Reo Māori
culturalContext: This lesson is deeply rooted in the local awa (stream) of Mangakōtukutuku, which flows through our rohe. Students will explore the pūrākau (traditional story) of Hinepūkohurangi, the mist maiden associated with this waterway, and connect it to contemporary kaitiakitanga practices of Ngāti Kahungunu. The learning will take place at actual sites along the awa where restoration work is occurring.
learningObjectives: Whakamahi i ngā kupu Māori matua e pā ana ki te kaitiakitanga me te taiao (use key Māori vocabulary related to guardianship and the environment)
Whakamārama i te pūrākau o Hinepūkohurangi me tōna hononga ki te awa o Mangakōtukutuku (explain the story of Hinepūkohurangi and its connection to our stream)
Waihanga i tētahi rautaki reo Māori hei whakatairanga i te kaitiakitanga o te awa (create a Māori language strategy to promote stream guardianship)
Whiriwhiri kōrero ki ngā kaikōrero Māori o te rohe mō ō rātou whakaaro kaitiaki (engage in conversation with local Māori speakers about their guardian perspectives)

activities.title: He Hīkoi, He Kōrero - Walk and Talk Along the Awa
activities.description: Students will walk the banks of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku with kaumātua Matua Wiremu Smith, learning the traditional names of native plants (harakeke, tī kōuka, mānuka) and aquatic life in te reo Māori while hearing the pūrākau of Hinepūkohurangi.

activities.title: Ngā Kupu Kaitiaki - Guardian Vocabulary Workshop
activities.description: Using specially created vocabulary cards featuring local flora and fauna, students will practice sentence structures for describing environmental observations (He aha tēnei? He pātiki. Kei te pai te pātiki? Kāo, kei te mate.)

activities.title: Te Whakawhiti Kōrero ki ngā Kaikōrero - Conversations with Knowledge Holders
activities.description: In small groups, students will prepare and conduct brief interviews with local kaitiaki from Ngāti Kahungunu's environmental team, using question frameworks in te reo to learn about current restoration efforts.

activities.title: He Rautaki Reo mō te Awa - Language Strategy for the Stream
activities.description: Students will create bilingual educational materials (signage, pamphlets, or social media content) using te reo Māori to promote awareness of the stream's cultural significance and protection needs.

resources: Matua Wiremu Smith (Ngāti Kahungunu kaumātua and storyteller)
Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku Restoration Project field site
'Ngā Manu o Te Awa' bilingual identification guide from Hawke's Bay Regional Council
Te Papakupu o Te Marautanga o Aotearoa (online Māori dictionary)

tasks: Create a 2-minute audio recording describing three aspects of the awa using appropriate te reo vocabulary
Develop a bilingual informational leaflet about kaitiakitanga of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
Participate in a simulated conversation with a community elder about stream protection

nzcAlignment: Te Reo Māori: Communicate about people, places, and things
Te Reo Māori: Use cultural knowledge to communicate appropriately
Social Sciences: Understand how people manage resources
Health and Physical Education: Community resources and environmental health
